titleStmt.title: Telegram from Basil Home Thomson, the Assistant Commissioner of Police, to Sir Neville Chamberlain, 8 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Basil Home Thomson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A telegram from the Assistant Commissioner to Inspector General Royal Irish Constabulary Sir Neville Francis Fitzgerald Chamberlain (1856-1944). The Assistant Commissioner is asking Chamberlain to take the necessary steps to monitor any post sent by Patrick Hastings, an inmate of Killadysert Workhouse. The request comes after Hastings sent several letters, described as 'insane' in nature, to the King.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6322/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
1.32 PARLIAMENT ST 20 INSPR GENERAL ROYAL IRISH CONSTY DUBLIN CASTLE My letter, 7th ulto. Your 21045,
Patrick Hastings continues address
letters to king. It is requested that
you kindly give directions to
stop this annoyance. Please wire
reply, ASSISTANT COMMISSIONER C I D NEW
, Scotland Yard.
Wired to C.I.D
Will receive immediate
attention as soon as case can
be traced.8/4 Added 'Please send
further particulars' 21045 Sort no.
For of 7th March was
addressed to Under Secy.
